stories.title: Kylie Kelce Calls Taylor Swift ‘Family’ After Wedding to Travis Kelce, and Says She’s Been ‘For Quite Some Time’
stories.summary: Kylie Kelce has referred to Taylor Swift as "family" after her brother-in-law Travis Kelce's wedding to the singer, stating she's been part of the family for quite some time. The ceremony took place on July 3 at New York's Madison Square Garden. Kelce expressed her happiness for the couple, urging fans to respect their privacy and seek details directly from Taylor and Travis.

stories.title: ‘Resident Evil’ Trailer: Zach Cregger’s Take On Iconic Vidgame Is Coming For You (And Comic-Con)
stories.summary: A new trailer has been released for the upcoming Resident Evil film, directed by Zach Cregger, which is set to hit theaters on September 18. The movie follows medical courier Bryan, played by Austin Abrams, as he navigates a nightmarish situation after a series of unfortunate events, including a car accident and a delivery gone wrong. The film promises to deliver a fresh spin on the iconic video game franchise with Easter eggs and a survival horror tone.

stories.title: Robin Williams defended Jumanji child stars Bradley Pierce and Kirsten Dunst on set
stories.summary: Robin Williams defended child stars Bradley Pierce and Kirsten Dunst on the set of Jumanji in 1995, intervening when producers attempted to make them work illegal overtime. The producers had demanded the children work beyond their permitted hours, despite Pierce struggling with a scene due to water and extensive makeup. Williams' actions ensured the children's safety and well-being, a testament to his protective nature and kindness.

stories.title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
stories.summary: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of its workers, approximately 93 employees, as the company adapts to the impact of artificial intelligence on its operations. This move follows a previous layoff of 17 percent in 2022 and the closure of offices in Dublin and Berlin. The company's CEO, Jack Conte, has emphasized Patreon's commitment to embracing AI tools to continue serving creators.

stories.title: Burnham pauses early prison release scheme
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has paused a plan to release thousands of prisoners early in England and Wales after strong criticism from victims' families. The scheme, set to start in September under the Sentencing Act, would have let some offenders out after serving a third of their sentence instead of half. The government now plans a review to address concerns and ensure public safety before any changes proceed.

stories.title: Modric, 40, signs new AC Milan deal
stories.summary: Luka Modric, 40, has signed a one-year contract extension with AC Milan, following a season where the team finished fifth in Serie A. Modric joined Milan last summer on a one-year deal with an option to extend, after leaving Real Madrid where he spent 13 years. He will look to bounce back from a disappointing season with AC Milan in the upcoming campaign.

stories.title: Corsair’s PC case with a panoramic glass design is $70 off
stories.summary: Corsair's PC case, the Frame 4500X RS, is now available for $119.99, a $70 discount from its usual price, at Amazon, Best Buy, and Corsair's website. This mid-tower case features a panoramic glass design, supports large motherboards and GPUs, and includes three fans with reverse rotors. The case's Infinirail system allows for easy fan installation and cable management.

stories.title: ‘Breaking Bad’ Star Giancarlo Esposito Signs With UTA for Representation (EXCLUSIVE)
stories.summary: Giancarlo Esposito, a six-time Emmy nominee, has signed with United Talent Agency for representation in all areas. The prolific actor will appear in several projects in the coming months, including the film "The Only Living Pickpocket in New York" and the Netflix series "The Gentlemen." He will also be seen in the animated film "The Cat in the Hat" and several other projects, including "Your Mother, Your Mother, Your Mother" and "All the Sinners Bleed," which are set to premiere in the coming months.

stories.title: Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated
stories.summary: Ukraine's ousted defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov has refused to accept an alternative post, insisting he wants his job back as defence minister. President Volodymyr Zelensky had offered Fedorov several positions, including deputy prime minister, but he declined. Mass protests continue in Ukraine, with some protesters vowing to keep demonstrating until Fedorov is reinstated as defence minister.

stories.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet saw its share price drop by over 7% on Thursday, while Tesla's stock fell 13.5%, as investors reacted to the companies' massive spending on artificial intelligence. Alphabet now expects to spend up to $205 billion this year, a $15 billion increase from its previous estimate, mainly on AI projects and infrastructure. This significant investment has raised concerns about when financial benefits will materialize from these costly AI endeavors.

stories.title: Russell proud of resilience after string of problems
stories.summary: Mercedes driver George Russell has narrowed his deficit to 25 points after a series of challenges in recent races, including engine-related issues in Belgium. Despite setbacks, Russell remains optimistic about his progress, citing improvements from Monaco to Silverstone. He now looks to build on this momentum ahead of the Hungarian Grand Prix.

stories.title: Wolves training cancelled after Arokodare refuses to leave pitch
stories.summary: Wolves striker Tolu Arokodare's training session was cancelled after he allegedly refused to leave the pitch, following a rejected bid from Fiorentina and an accepted offer from Turkish club Trabzonspor. Arokodare has denied the allegations, stating he was simply preparing for his role in the team. The incident marks the latest controversy in Arokodare's career at Molineux, following a disciplinary action in April.

stories.title: Final Coyote vs. Acme trailer drops at SDCC
stories.summary: The final trailer for Coyote vs. Acme drops at San Diego Comic-Con today, with the animated film set for theaters on August 28, 2026. The movie follows Wile E. Coyote’s legal battle against Acme Corporation after repeated failures to catch the Road Runner. Warner Bros. plans a wide release following the SDCC premiere.

stories.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says Saudi Arabia must recognize Israel to secure a U.S. nuclear energy deal. The agreement, which bars uranium enrichment, hinges on Riyadh joining the Abraham Accords. Saudi officials have not yet responded to the condition.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

stories.title: ‘The Strange Architect,’ New Series by ‘Annihilation’ Author Jeff VanderMeer, Sells to Farrar, Straus and Giroux in Seven-Figure Deal (EXCLUSIVE)
stories.summary: Farrar, Straus and Giroux has acquired a new four-book series by bestselling author Jeff VanderMeer in a seven-figure deal. The first book, How to Be Free, will launch in winter 2029. The series follows Arthur Bream, who builds houses with a mysterious symbol after his daughter’s disappearance.

stories.title: ‘Myron Bolitar’ Series Adds 10 To Cast, Including Melissa Benoist, Bebe Neuwirth, Jamie Hector, Quincy Isaiah
stories.summary: Netflix’s “Myron Bolitar” series has added ten new cast members, including Melissa Benoist, Bebe Neuwirth, and Jamie Hector. Ra’Mya Latiah Aikens joins as a series regular while nine others will appear in recurring guest roles. Production is set to begin later this year.

stories.title: ‘We Will All Die From Ebola Here’: Virus Stalks Crowded Camps in Congo
stories.summary: There are a million people displaced by violence in the epicenter. They live in dangerous conditions and are fighting the virus with very little clean water.

stories.title: Chaos Breaks Out at Border Crossings With Iraq
stories.summary: Videos verified by The New York Times show border crossings in southern Iraq becoming the latest target in the hostilities in the Middle East.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

stories.title: House Again Rebukes Trump on Iran War Powers Vote
stories.summary: For the second time, a few House Republicans joined Democrats to back a measure calling on the president to seek authorization for the war.

stories.title: The Tate Brothers Are Losing Right-Wing Allies After Their Arrest
stories.summary: After Andrew and Tristan Tate, prominent leaders of the so-called manosphere, were arrested in Miami, many of their onetime allies on the American right pulled back support.

stories.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
stories.summary: Petrol pump thieves are exploiting a loophole in the payment system, allowing them to fill up and flee without paying. In the UK, it's estimated that thousands of litres of fuel are stolen every year, with some garages reporting losses of up to £10,000. To combat this issue, some experts are advocating for a system where pumps are only activated once a payment method is confirmed.

stories.title: ‘Odyssey’ Popcorn Buckets Help Fuel a $100 Million Market
stories.summary: Once, a tub of popcorn was just a tub of popcorn. Now, ‘collectible concession vessels’ could make one chain more than $100 million.

stories.title: BBC Inside Science
stories.summary: The US military will begin screening military personnel aged 30 and older for testosterone deficiency, with those found to have low levels offered voluntary hormone replacement therapy. The move aims to maintain a "healthy, capable, and decisively dominant fighting force". The screenings are expected to impact thousands of service members.

stories.title: Crews Move Artemis IV Liquid Hydrogen Tank
stories.summary: Crews at NASA's Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans have successfully transported the 130-foot-tall Artemis IV liquid hydrogen tank to a detached test building on May 15, 2026. This tank will form part of the core stage for the SLS rocket, providing thousands of gallons of super-cold propellant to one of four RS-25 engines. The Artemis IV mission is a crucial step towards NASA's plans for a sustainable presence on the lunar surface.

stories.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
stories.summary: A study of 3.1 million Danish participants has found that prolonged exposure to road traffic noise may increase the risk of Parkinson's disease by 3% for every 11.5 decibel rise in noise level. The study, published in Jama Neurology, followed participants for 18 years and suggests that having a quiet part of the home may mitigate this risk. The findings highlight a potential link between noise exposure and the development of Parkinson's disease.

stories.title: Poorest children in England 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections
stories.summary: Children in England’s poorest areas are 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections than those in wealthier regions, new NHS data shows. In 2024-25, 1,941 out of every 100,000 children in the most deprived areas were admitted for respiratory illnesses compared to 1,380 in the least deprived. Experts warn air pollution and deprivation are key drivers, with calls for urgent public health action.
